summary: Gruber announces a special remote video episode of The Talk Show for WWDC 2020, teasing unnamed special guests while noting the unusual pandemic circumstances. He then discusses how the pandemic has affected his advertising business, observing that DF's website sponsorships have held up better than podcast sponsorships — a reversal of the pre-pandemic trend. He attributes this to DF's website sponsors being smaller indie companies with a direct understanding of the audience, while podcast sponsors tend to come through traditional ad agencies tied to the broader market. He pitches sponsorship openings for both the WWDC show and regular slots, encouraging indie companies to sponsor the podcast at reduced rates.
key_points: The Talk Show will have a special remote video episode for WWDC 2020 with unnamed special guests, adapting the traditional live format to pandemic conditions
Pre-pandemic, podcast ad revenue was outpacing website sponsorships, but post-pandemic that trend reversed, with DF website spots selling out further ahead
DF website sponsorships function as a unique niche market driven by indie companies who understand the specific audience, unlike typical web advertising with JavaScript tracking and cookies
Podcast sponsorships are more tied to the general advertising market through agencies, making them more vulnerable to broad economic downturns
Gruber's 2008-09 recession experience showed DF sponsorship revenue actually rose because the site was more tied to the iPhone/App Store boom than the general ad market
money_quotes: Advertising is always the first budget to take a hit in a recession. That's always been true, and I suspect always will be true, not matter how the advertising industry changes in terms of media and technology.
Try explaining to a mass market ad agency what an RSS sponsorship is, or why DF web ads don't allow any sort of JavaScript tracking or cookies — trust me, it's not easy.
Basically that means DF weekly sponsorships are more independent, almost a market unto itself, whereas The Talk Show sponsorships are much more a part of the general advertising market.
Everyone needs a mattress at some point.

DF sponsorship revenue actually rose significantly during that recession, because DF was more affected by the early go-go years of the iPhone and App Store than it was the broad mass market decline in ad spending.
key_insight: Niche, audience-aware sponsorships built on direct relationships prove more recession-resilient than agency-driven advertising tied to the broader market.
